channel.title: Optimization on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Optimization on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted Hyperparameter Optimization: Optuna vs Ray Tune vs Hyperopt Guide 2026
item.description: Finding the right hyperparameters for a model can make the difference between mediocre and state-of-the-art performance. Manual tuning is slow and error-prone. Automated hyperparameter optimization (HPO) frameworks systematically search the parameter space, finding better configurations with fewer training runs.

item.title: Self-Hosted Database Configuration Tuning: MySQLTuner vs pgTune vs pgtune-ng (2026)
item.description: Database performance tuning is one of the most impactful yet often overlooked aspects of self-hosted infrastructure. A database running with default configuration parameters typically uses only a fraction of available CPU, memory, and I/O resources. Proper tuning can improve query throughput by 2-10x and reduce latency dramatically.

item.title: Dive vs SlimToolkit vs Hadolint: Container Image Optimization Guide 2026
item.description: Every Docker image you build carries hidden costs. Bloated layers slow down CI/CD pipelines, waste registry storage, increase attack surfaces, and bloat deployment times. For teams running hundreds of microservices, a poorly optimized image can add gigabytes of unnecessary data to every deployment.
